steps.title: Use the parperp shortcut: radius slope then negative reciprocal
steps.content:
The radius goes from center $(-1, 1)$ to tangent point $(5, -4)$:
$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-4 - 1}{5 - (-1)} = \frac{-5}{6}$$
The tangent is perpendicular, so its slope is the negative reciprocal:
$$m_{\text{tangent}} = \frac{6}{5}$$

steps.title: Write the tangent line and identify the correct point
steps.content:
Line through $(5, -4)$ with slope $\frac{6}{5}$: $y = \frac{6}{5}x - 10$.
Test $(10, 2)$: $\frac{6}{5}(10) - 10 = 12 - 10 = 2$ ✓
That matches choice C — C is correct.
